summary: Gepotidacin does not activate bacterial topoisomerase—it instead selectively inhibits bacterial topoisomerase IV and DNA gyrase. There is no evidence supporting its ability to stimulate or enhance enzyme function; all findings point to direct inhibition causing cellular toxicity through disrupted DNA processes.
